title: Retain the Message: - Summarize: Write or Say the Main Idea in Your Own
subtitle: Focus on the Message, Not the Words
description:
To retain the message:
- Summarize: Write or say the main idea in your own words.
- Look for patterns: Identify recurring themes or key points.
- Practice context recall: Remember when and why something was said, not just how.

Example: After a meeting, jot down the core decisions and action points instead of trying to recall word-for-word what was said.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Pause and Summarize
howTo.summary: After a conversation or meeting, take 60 seconds to pause. Write or say one sentence that captures the main idea in your own words.

howTo.title: Add Context
howTo.summary: Include one contextual tag that identifies who said it, when it happened, or why it matters. This helps anchor the memory.

howTo.title: Assign Actions
howTo.summary: If there are any actions to be taken, write them down and assign an owner along with a due date. This ensures accountability.

howTo.title: Review Patterns
howTo.summary: Look back over your recent summaries to identify recurring themes or key points. This helps reinforce memory and understanding.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your summaries and actions in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/meeting-message-recall/.
